scenario_blurb: Stage IIIB non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) with EGFR L858R mutation in a 62-year-old former smoker. Initially treated with osimertinib with good partial response lasting 14 months, then develops progression with T790M resistance mutation and new brain metastases. Undergoes stereotactic radiosurgery and switches to platinum-based chemotherapy with eventual transition to clinical trial.

Triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) stage IIB in a 45-year-old woman, diagnosed after palpable mass and core needle biopsy showing grade 3 invasive ductal carcinoma, ER/PR/HER2 negative, Ki-67 85%. Treated with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense AC followed by paclitaxel, then surgery. Mixed pathologic response with residual disease, followed by adjuvant capecitabine and pembrolizumab maintenance.

Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation and synchronous liver metastases in a 58-year-old male. Initial treatment with FOLFOX plus bevacizumab for 12 cycles with partial response, then maintenance 5-FU/bevacizumab. Progression at 10 months with new lung nodules, switched to FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab. Develops oxaliplatin neuropathy and treatment-related hypertension.

Metastatic melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation in a 55-year-old, presenting with widespread cutaneous, lymph node, and hepatic metastases. Initially treated with dabrafenib/trametinib combination with rapid response but progression at 8 months. Switched to ipilimumab/nivolumab combination immunotherapy, experiencing grade 3 colitis requiring steroids, then durable partial response on nivolumab maintenance.

Castration-resistant prostate cancer in a 71-year-old with germline BRCA2 loss, rising PSA on ADT + abiraterone. Treated with enzalutamide with PSA response for 6 months, then progression with new bone metastases. Switched to olaparib with PSA decline. Develops anemia requiring transfusions and eventually progresses with visceral metastases.

High-grade serous ovarian cancer FIGO stage IIIC in a 52-year-old with germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant. Presents with ascites and peritoneal carcinomatosis. Undergoes primary debulking surgery achieving R0 resection, followed by 6 cycles of carboplatin/paclitaxel with CA-125 normalization. Started on niraparib maintenance therapy. Develops thrombocytopenia requiring dose reduction.

Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with FLT3-ITD mutation (high allelic ratio) in a 48-year-old, presenting with leukocytosis, anemia, and gingival bleeding. Treated with 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin, achieves CR after first induction. MRD negative by flow cytometry. Proceeds to matched unrelated donor allogeneic stem cell transplant with mild chronic GVHD.

Clear cell renal cell carcinoma stage IV with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%) in a 63-year-old, presenting with large right renal mass, retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and pulmonary metastases. Undergoes cytoreductive nephrectomy. First-line ipilimumab/nivolumab with mixed response. Progression at 9 months, switched to cabozantinib with stable disease. Manages TKI-related hand-foot syndrome and diarrhea.

Locally advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma (borderline resectable) in a 66-year-old with tumor abutting the SMA and SMV. CA 19-9 elevated to 1200. Treated with neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX for 8 cycles with CA 19-9 decline to 85 and partial response on imaging. Then chemoradiation with capecitabine. Successfully undergoes Whipple procedure with negative margins. Adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine complicated by pancreatic fistula.

HPV-positive (p16+) oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (base of tongue) stage III (T2N1M0) in a 52-year-old nonsmoker. Treated with definitive concurrent cisplatin and intensity-modulated radiation therapy. Develops grade 3 mucositis and requires PEG tube for nutrition support. Achieves complete response. Post-treatment surveillance with consideration of de-escalation in the adjuvant setting.
